proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 37 anonymized features, the binary indicator feature_006 (prevalence 30%) is associated with shorter mean pfs_months than feature_006=0.
The continuous feature_012 (range 30-90) is positively correlated with pfs_months in the full cohort.
The 3-level feature_005 shows a monotone gradient in pfs_months: pfs_months(level 0) > pfs_months(level 1) > pfs_months(level 2).

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; a=df.loc[df['feature_006']==0,'pfs_months']; b=df.loc[df['feature_006']==1,'pfs_months']; stats.ttest_ind(a,b,equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 5.153 (feature_006=0, n=35031) vs 3.617 (feature_006=1, n=14969); Welch t-test p<1e-300. feature_006=1 is associated with a 1.54-month reduction in PFS.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.7015 (p<1e-300). pfs_months rises monotonically across feature_012 bins: <40 yr -> 0.56 mo; 40-50 -> 1.52 mo; 50-60 -> 3.06 mo; 60-70 -> 4.67 mo; 70-80 -> 6.32 mo; 80+ -> 8.20 mo. Each unit feature_012 ~ +0.176 months PFS in OLS.

analyses.code: stats.f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_005']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in [0,1,2]])
analyses.result_summary: Means 5.640 (level 0, n=17592), 4.444 (level 1, n=24971), 3.289 (level 2, n=7437). One-way ANOVA F=2860.75, p<1e-300. Strong monotone gradient consistent with stage-like ordinal severity marker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature_013 (prevalence 35%) is associated with longer mean pfs_months than feature_013=0 in the full cohort.
The rare binary feature_001 (prevalence 10%) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_001=0.
Each remaining binary feature (feature_011, 018, 019, 020, 022, 023, 028, 029, 030, etc.) was screened for a marginal association with pfs_months by Welch t-test.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_013']==0,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_013']==1,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs 4.310 (n=32572) vs 5.408 (n=17428); Welch t-test p<1e-300. feature_013=1 confers a +1.10-month PFS gain on average; this is the largest positive marginal effect among all binary features.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_001']==0,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_001']==1,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs 4.800 (feature_001=0, n=44910) vs 3.750 (feature_001=1, n=5090); p=1.45e-178. feature_001=1 is associated with a 1.05-month reduction.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: ttest_ind(...)
analyses.result_summary: Significant marginal effects (signed diff for feature=1 vs 0): feature_029 +0.568 (p=3.7e-143), feature_028 -0.559 (p=6.1e-144), feature_018 -0.459 (p=6.1e-70), feature_023 +0.362 (p=5.8e-59), feature_016 +0.114 (p=1.0e-6). Non-significant: feature_011, 030, 019, 015, 008, 009, 026, 020, 022, 032 (all |diff|<0.13).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous features, only a subset (feature_004, feature_024, feature_033, feature_031, feature_027, feature_010, feature_007, feature_037) is associated with pfs_months at p<0.05.
Most binary features whose univariate p-values are tiny remain independently significant after adjustment for all other features in a single multivariable OLS model.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: stats.pearsonr(df[c], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r values vs pfs_months: feature_012 +0.702 (p=0), feature_004 -0.116 (p=4e-148), feature_024 +0.100 (p=9e-112), feature_033 -0.091 (p=3e-93), feature_031 -0.016, feature_027 -0.013, feature_010 -0.013, feature_007 +0.010, feature_037 -0.009. The remaining ten continuous features (feature_021, 003, 025, 014, 034, 002, 035, 017, 036, 015) have |r|<0.005 and p>0.30.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X = pd.get_dummies(df[feat_cols], columns=['feature_005'], drop_first=True).astype(float); X = sm.add_constant(X); m = sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], X).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.7923. Independent coefficients (effect on pfs_months): feature_005_2=-2.33, feature_006=-1.55, feature_005_1=-1.19, feature_013=+1.10, feature_001=-0.98, feature_028=-0.58, feature_029=+0.53, feature_024=+0.48 (per unit), feature_018=-0.47, feature_012=+0.176 (per yr), feature_004=-0.077 (per unit), feature_033=-0.019 (per unit). feature_023's marginal +0.36 collapses to +0.014 (n.s.) after adjustment, indicating confounding with the other markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment assignment to feature_013 is independent of all other recorded features (i.e., feature_013 was randomized rather than confounded with prognostic markers).

analyses.code: sm.Logit(df['feature_013'], sm.add_constant(X_other)).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression of feature_013 on all 36 other features: no coefficient is significant at p<0.05; the smallest p-value is 0.053 (feature_027). feature_013 prevalence is 0.347-0.355 across every stratum of feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, feature_028, feature_018, feature_029, feature_023, feature_012 quartiles, and feature_033 quartiles. Conclusion: feature_013 is essentially randomized at ~35% prevalence, supporting causal interpretation of its effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013's +1.1-month PFS benefit is uniform across the strata of feature_005 (the strongest prognostic marker).
feature_013's PFS benefit is uniform across feature_006 strata.
feature_013's PFS benefit is uniform across feature_001 strata.

analyses.code: for v in [0,1,2]: stats.ttest_ind(... feature_005==v, by feature_013)
analyses.result_summary: feature_005=0: TE=+1.115 (p=6e-156); feature_005=1: TE=+1.093 (p=7e-218); feature_005=2: TE=+1.149 (p=7e-77). Treatment effect of feature_013 is essentially constant (~+1.1 mo) across all three feature_005 strata. Interaction test in OLS: feature_013xfeature_005==1 p=0.82; feature_013xfeature_005==2 p=0.41. No effect modification by feature_005.

analyses.code: feature_013 stratified by feature_006
analyses.result_summary: feature_006=0: TE=+1.096 (p=4e-295); feature_006=1: TE=+1.102 (p=1e-135). Interaction coefficient +0.006, p=0.901. No modification.

analyses.code: feature_013 stratified by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=0: TE=+1.099 (p=0); feature_001=1: TE=+1.058 (p=2.5e-41). Interaction coefficient -0.041, p=0.586. No modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all binary features, exhaustive screening of feature_013xMfeature interaction terms in OLS reveals significant effect modifiers; we conjecture the strongest are feature_028, feature_029, feature_018, feature_023.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: sm.OLS(pfs ~ T + M + T:M)
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature interaction screen for feature_013. Significant modifiers (TM coefficient on natural scale): feature_028 -1.623 (p=4e-255), feature_029 +1.594 (p=9e-229), feature_018 -1.364 (p=2e-118), feature_023 +1.093 (p=3e-117), feature_016 +0.419 (p=9e-19), feature_019 -0.134 (p=4e-3). All other binary features (feature_011, 030, 020, 026, 015, 001, 009, 022, 032, 008, 006) have |TM|<0.16 and p>0.27. The four strongest modifiers (28, 29, 18, 23) co-define the responder subgroup explored next.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the cell defined by feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_029=1, the treatment effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is greatly enlarged (>2 mo) compared to the overall +1.1.
Within the cells where feature_028=1 OR feature_018=1, the treatment effect of feature_013 is approximately zero.

analyses.code: for f28 in [0,1]: for f18 in [0,1]: for f29 in [0,1]: t-test
df.query('feature_028==1 or feature_018==1') ttest by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: n=23240; TE = +0.018 months (p=0.535). In any patient with feature_028=1 or feature_018=1, treatment with feature_013 yields no detectable PFS benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028, feature_018, and feature_029 have no main effect on pfs_months in untreated patients; their apparent overall associations are entirely mediated by their role as modifiers of feature_013.
feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, feature_012, feature_004, and feature_024 are pure prognostic markers - their associations with pfs_months persist in untreated (feature_013=0) patients.

analyses.code: in df[df['feature_013']==0]: ttest by feature_028, feature_018, feature_029
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_013=0 patients (n=32572): feature_028 effect = +0.008 (p=0.74); feature_018 effect = +0.015 (p=0.64); feature_029 effect = +0.011 (p=0.68); feature_023 effect = -0.020 (p=0.41). Among feature_013=1 patients: feature_028 = -1.615 (p=0), feature_018 = -1.349 (p=3e-180), feature_029 = +1.605 (p=0). These four features are purely PREDICTIVE biomarkers of feature_013 sensitivity, with no prognostic role of their own.

analyses.code: in df[df['feature_013']==0]: associations of feature_005/006/001/012/004/024
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_013=0: feature_005 ANOVA F=2736 (p=0); feature_006 diff = -1.538 (p=0); feature_001 diff = -1.029 (p=4e-155); feature_012 r=+0.829 (p=0); feature_004 r=-0.144 (p=0); feature_024 r=+0.119 (p=0). All retain their effect direction and magnitude in untreated patients - they are pure prognostic markers, independent of treatment assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, the apparent main-effect association of feature_033 with pfs_months reflects an interaction with feature_013, not a true prognostic effect: in untreated patients feature_033 is uncorrelated with pfs_months, while in treated patients there is a clear inverse association.
Within the responder subgroup (feature_028=0, feature_018=0, feature_029=1) treated with feature_013, increasing feature_033 monotonically erodes the treatment benefit.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr(df_untreated['feature_033'], df_untreated['pfs']); stats.pearsonr(df_treated['feature_033'], df_treated['pfs'])
analyses.result_summary: feature_033 vs pfs_months in feature_013=0: r=-0.002 (p=0.68); in feature_013=1: r=-0.220 (p=9e-190); in treated responders only: r=-0.517 (p=0). Confirms feature_033 is a PREDICTIVE biomarker (modifier of feature_013 effect), not prognostic. Interaction TM coefficient (per SD): -0.637, p=1e-174.

analyses.code: in responders, feature_013 effect by feature_033 decile
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, treatment effect of feature_013 by feature_033 decile (deciles 0-9 with mean f033 of 3.7, 5.9, 7.6, 9.3, 11.3, 13.4, 16.1, 19.7, 25.2, 43.2): TE = +4.92, +4.88, +5.03, +5.01, +4.89, +3.97, +0.17, -0.07, -0.23, +0.16 months. A sharp threshold near feature_033 ~ 14-15 separates large benefit (~+5 mo) from no benefit. Within-responder T*feature_033 standardized interaction = -0.135 per SD (p=0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The univariate +0.36-month effect of feature_023 entirely disappears in the multivariable model and is explained by joint distribution with the responder subgroup.
feature_016's apparent effect modification (+0.42 in the bivariate screen) loses significance when adjusted for the dominant feature_028/018/029 modifiers.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + f028 + f018 + f029 + f023 + f016 + f033 + interactions
analyses.result_summary: Joint model with all candidate modifiers: T=+0.795 (intercept treatment effect), TxF028=-1.589 (p=3e-263), TxF018=-1.337 (p=7e-109), TxF029=+1.466 (p=1e-116), TxF033(z)=-0.630 (p=9e-186), TxF023=+0.121 (p=0.047), TxF016=-0.0004 (p=0.99). feature_023 contributes a small residual modifier (~+0.12 mo) but loses essentially all of its bivariate strength once feature_028/018/029 are in the model. feature_016's effect modifier is fully explained away.

analyses.code: OLS as above (feature_016 interaction p=0.99 in joint model)
analyses.result_summary: TxF016 = -0.0004 (p=0.99) in joint model. Confirms feature_016 is not a real effect modifier; its bivariate signal (TM=+0.42) was driven by correlation with the true responder markers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Aside from feature_013, no other binary feature behaves like a treatment with a usable randomized effect on pfs_months. Specifically, feature_029 and feature_023 have no PFS effect when feature_013=0.

analyses.code: in feature_013=0 stratum: t-tests for each binary feature; verify zero effect
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_013=0 patients, every binary feature OTHER than the prognostic ones (006, 001) is null: feature_028 +0.008 (p=0.74), feature_018 +0.015 (p=0.64), feature_029 +0.011 (p=0.68), feature_023 -0.020 (p=0.41), feature_016/019/020/022/030/032/008/009/015/011/026 all |diff|<0.06 with p>0.20. No alternative treatment is hidden in the data.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Optimal threshold for feature_033 in the responder definition is approximately 14: at feature_033 <= 14 the treatment effect is ~+5 mo, while at feature_033 > 14 (within the otherwise-responder set) the effect is ~0.

analyses.code: for thr in [10,12,13,14,15,16,17,18,20]: TE in responders with feature_033<=thr
analyses.result_summary: Within responders, treatment effect by feature_033 threshold (in / out): thr=10 (+4.96 / +1.57); thr=12 (+4.96 / +0.91); thr=13 (+4.92 / +0.49); thr=14 (+4.94 / +0.024); thr=15 (+4.65 / +0.021); thr=18 (+4.09 / -0.06). The cleanest break is at thr=14: in-group TE=+4.94, complementary out-group TE=+0.024 (p=0.85). This identifies the maximally enriched responder cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: Among patients with feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_029=1 AND feature_033<=14 (a 'biomarker-defined responder' subgroup, ~22% of the cohort), the treatment feature_013 increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months relative to control. In every other patient (~78% of the cohort), feature_013 has no detectable effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: mask=(f028==0)&(f018==0)&(f029==1)&(f033<=14); compare PFS by feature_013 within and outside mask
analyses.result_summary: Refined responder subgroup (n=10828, 21.7% of cohort): mean PFS untreated 4.285 (n=6975) vs treated 9.228 (n=3853); treatment effect +4.943 months (p<1e-300; Welch). Complementary 'non-responder' set (n=39172, 78.3%): treatment effect +0.0071 months (p=0.75). Joint OLS confirms: T_responder coefficient +2.92 (p=0), T_responder_f033 coefficient -1.68 per SD (p=0); main T effect (in non-responders) +0.0004 (p=0.96). Final R^2 of the prognostic+treatment model: 0.929.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (which behaves like a prognostic 'older = longer PFS' feature, possibly age in this synthetic dataset) does not modify the treatment effect of feature_013.
Within responders, the treatment effect is preserved across all strata of feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, and feature_012.

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T + f012 + T:f012 (per SD)
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature_012 interaction (per SD): TM = -0.032, p=0.044. The interaction is statistically significant by virtue of N=50000 but trivially small (~0.03 mo per SD of feature_012). Treatment effect is essentially constant across feature_012 strata.

analyses.code: Within refined responders, stratify TE by f005, f006, f001, f012 quartiles
analyses.result_summary: Within the refined responder subgroup, TE remains in the +4.5 to +5.3 month range across feature_005 levels, feature_006 strata, feature_001 strata, and feature_012 quartiles (per stratified t-tests). The treatment benefit is robust to other prognostic factors.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for the responder/non-responder distinction and the prognostic features identified, the residual variance in pfs_months is small (R^2 of the joint model ~ 0.93).

analyses.code: OLS pfs ~ T*responder + T*responder*f033c + f012 + f005_1 + f005_2 + f006 + f001 + f004 + f024
analyses.result_summary: Final compact joint model fits with R^2=0.929 on 50000 observations. Coefficients: const=-7.21; T (in non-responders)=+0.0004 (p=0.96); responder=+0.0002 (p=0.98); T_responder=+2.92 (p=0); T_responder_f033 (per SD)=-1.68 (p=0); f012=+0.176 (p=0); f005_1=-1.19 (p=0); f005_2=-2.33 (p=0); f006=-1.55 (p=0); f001=-0.96 (p=0); f004=-0.077 (p=0); f024=+0.49 (p=0). The model captures the treatment-biomarker structure plus the major prognostic gradient.
